---
title: "Untitled"
output: html_document
date: "2025-03-19"
---

```{r}
library(ggplot2)
library(lmerTest)
library(lme4)
```

```{r}
data <- read.csv("/Users/kalle/Desktop/gender_slur_paper/gender_slur_s1_data.csv")
```

```{r}
# Count frequency of each list across all rows
all_rows_count <- table(data$List, useNA = "ifany")
print("Frequency of each list across all rows:")
print(all_rows_count)

# Count frequency of each list by unique submission_id (participants)
# This gives us how many participants are assigned to each list
participant_list_count <- table(unique(data[, c("submission_id", "List")])$List, useNA = "ifany")
print("Frequency of each list by unique participants (submission_id):")
print(participant_list_count)
```

```{r}
# Remove rows where submission_id equals "TEST"
data <- data[data$submission_id != "TEST", ]
```


```{r}
# Get unique participants with their ages
unique_participants <- unique(data[, c("submission_id", "age")])

# Calculate mean and standard deviation of age
mean_age <- mean(unique_participants$age, na.rm = TRUE)
sd_age <- sd(unique_participants$age, na.rm = TRUE)

# Display results
cat("Mean age:", round(mean_age, 2), "\n")
cat("Standard deviation of age:", round(sd_age, 2), "\n")
cat("Number of unique participants:", nrow(unique_participants), "\n")
```



```{r}
# 4. Remove participants who failed control checks
control1_failures <- data[data$item_number == "Control1" & data$response_type == "Group", ]
control2_failures <- data[data$item_number == "Control2" & data$response_type == "Individual", ]

failed_submission_ids <- unique(c(control1_failures$submission_id, control2_failures$submission_id))

control1_submission_id_ids <- unique(control1_failures$submission_id)
control2_submission_id_ids <- unique(control2_failures$submission_id)
all_failed_submission_id_ids <- unique(c(control1_submission_id_ids, control2_submission_id_ids))

cat("Number of participants who failed Control1:", length(unique(control1_failures$submission_id)), "\n")
cat("Number of participants who failed Control2:", length(unique(control2_failures$submission_id)), "\n")
cat("Total number of participants who failed at least one control:", length(failed_submission_ids), "\n")

cat("\nsubmission_id IDs of participants who failed Control1:\n")
print(control1_submission_id_ids)

cat("\nsubmission_id IDs of participants who failed Control2:\n")
print(control2_submission_id_ids)

cat("\nAll submission_id IDs of participants who failed at least one control:\n")
print(all_failed_submission_id_ids)

total_participants_before <- length(unique(data$submission_id))
data_clean <- data[!data$submission_id %in% failed_submission_ids, ]
total_participants_after <- length(unique(data_clean$submission_id))

cat("\nRemoved", total_participants_before - total_participants_after, "participants\n")
cat("Participants remaining:", total_participants_after, "\n")

cat("Rows before:", nrow(data), "\n")
cat("Rows after:", nrow(data_clean), "\n")
cat("Rows removed:", nrow(data) - nrow(data_clean), "\n")
```

```{r}
# 6. Identify and remove participants who chose "Group" for multiple filler items
filler_group_responses <- data_clean[grepl("^filler", data_clean$item_number) & 
                                     data_clean$response_type == "Group", ]

filler_group_counts <- table(filler_group_responses$submission_id)

problematic_participants <- names(filler_group_counts[filler_group_counts > 1])

cat("Number of participants who chose 'Group' for more than one filler item:", 
    length(problematic_participants), "\n")

problematic_submission_id_ids <- unique(data_clean$submission_id[data_clean$submission_id %in% problematic_participants])
cat("\nsubmission_id IDs of participants who chose 'Group' for multiple fillers:\n")
print(problematic_submission_id_ids)

participants_before_filler_check <- length(unique(data_clean$submission_id))

data_clean_rows_before <- nrow(data_clean)
data_clean <- data_clean[!data_clean$submission_id %in% problematic_participants, ]
participants_after_filler_check <- length(unique(data_clean$submission_id))

cat("\nRemoved", participants_before_filler_check - participants_after_filler_check, 
    "additional participants who chose 'Group' for multiple fillers\n")
cat("Participants remaining after all exclusions:", participants_after_filler_check, "\n")

cat("Rows after control exclusions:", data_clean_rows_before, "\n")
cat("Rows after filler exclusions:", nrow(data_clean), "\n")
cat("Additional rows removed:", data_clean_rows_before - nrow(data_clean), "\n")
```

```{r}
# 7. Remove filler and control items to create experimental dataset
data_exp <- data_clean[grep("^item", data_clean$item_number), ]
```

```{r}
# Load necessary libraries
library(dplyr)
library(tidyr)

# Calculate summary statistics for response_type based on pejorative_type, speaker, and participant_gender
response_summary <- data_exp %>%
  # Group by the three factors
  group_by(pejorative_type, speaker, participant_gender, response_type) %>%
  # Count observations in each group
  summarise(
    n = n(),
    .groups = "drop"
  ) %>%
  # Calculate total observations for each combination of the three factors
  group_by(pejorative_type, speaker, participant_gender) %>%
  mutate(
    total = sum(n),
    rel_freq = n / total,
    se = sqrt((rel_freq * (1 - rel_freq)) / total),
    ci_lower = pmax(0, rel_freq - 1.96 * se),
    ci_upper = pmin(1, rel_freq + 1.96 * se)
  ) %>%
  # Arrange the results for better readability
  arrange(pejorative_type, speaker, participant_gender, response_type)

# View the summary statistics
print(response_summary, n = Inf)

# You can also create a wide-format table for easier comparisons
response_summary_wide <- response_summary %>%
  select(pejorative_type, speaker, participant_gender, response_type, n, rel_freq) %>%
  pivot_wider(
    names_from = response_type,
    values_from = c(n, rel_freq),
    values_fill = list(n = 0, rel_freq = 0)
  )

# View the wide-format table
print(response_summary_wide, n = Inf)
```

```{r}
# Assuming you already created response_summary from previous code
# Filter for just the "Group" response type (or adjust to match your actual response type category)
plot_data <- response_summary %>%
  filter(response_type == "Group") %>%
  rename(group_pct = rel_freq) %>%
  mutate(
    # Convert to percentage
    group_pct = group_pct * 100,
    ci = 1.96 * se * 100 # Converting SE to 95% CI in percentage points
  )
# Create the publication-ready plot with your requested changes
ggplot(plot_data, aes(x = pejorative_type, y = group_pct, 
                       color = participant_gender, 
                       group = participant_gender)) +
  # Add error bars
  geom_errorbar(aes(ymin = group_pct - ci, ymax = group_pct + ci), 
                width = 0.1, position = position_dodge(0.03)) +
  geom_line(size = 0.8) +
  geom_point(size = 3) +
  # Use a cleaner facet layout with a better label
  facet_wrap(~speaker, labeller = labeller(speaker = c(
    "female" = "Female Speaker",
    "male" = "Male Speaker"
  ))) +
  # Use descriptive labels with proper capitalization
  labs(
    x = "Pejorative",
    y = "Group-Directed Responses",
    color = "Participant"
  ) +
  # Use a professional color palette
  scale_color_brewer(palette = "Set1", 
                    labels = c("female" = "Female", "male" = "Male")) +
 # Format y-axis as percentage with breaks at 20% increments
  scale_y_continuous(
    labels = function(x) paste0(x, "%"),
    breaks = seq(0, 100, by = 20)
  ) +
  # Rename x-axis labels if needed
  scale_x_discrete(labels = c(
    "SLUR" = "Slur",
    "GENDERED" = "Gendered Term",
    "NEUTRAL" = "Neutral Term"
  )) +
  # Use a clean, professional theme
  theme_bw() +
  theme(
    axis.text = element_text(size = 10),
    axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 0, hjust = 0.5),  # Changed to horizontal (angle = 0)
    legend.position = "right",
    legend.background = element_rect(fill = NA, color = NA),
    strip.background = element_rect(fill = "gray95"),
    strip.text = element_text(size = 11),
    panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
    panel.grid.major.x = element_blank(),
    panel.border = element_rect(color = "black", fill = NA, linewidth = 0.5)
  )
```

```{r}
# Convert existing variables to factors
data_exp$response_type <- factor(data_exp$response_type)  # Keep original values
data_exp$pejorative_type <- factor(data_exp$pejorative_type)
data_exp$participant_gender <- factor(data_exp$participant_gender)
data_exp$speaker <- factor(data_exp$speaker)
data_exp$submission_id <- factor(data_exp$submission_id)


# Create binary response variable
data_exp$response_binary <- ifelse(data_exp$response_type == "Group", 1, 0)

# Now run the model
model_moderate <- glmer(
  response_binary ~ pejorative_type * participant_gender * speaker + 
                   (1 + pejorative_type | submission_id) + 
                   (1 | item_number),
  data = data_exp,
  family = binomial,
  control = glmerControl(optimizer = "bobyqa", optCtrl = list(maxfun = 50000))
)
summary(model_moderate)
```

```{r}
library(ggplot2)
library(dplyr)
library(tidyr)

# Aggregate data: Percentage of group responses per participant & pejorative type
participant_summary <- data_exp %>%
  group_by(submission_id, pejorative_type) %>%
  summarize(group_response_rate = mean(response_binary) * 100, .groups = "drop")
```


```{r}
library(ggplot2)

ggplot(participant_summary, aes(x = pejorative_type, y = group_response_rate)) +
  geom_boxplot(outlier.shape = NA) +
  geom_jitter(width = 0.2, alpha = 0.5, color = "blue") +
  labs(
    x = "Pejorative Type",
    y = "Group-Directed Responses by Participant"
  ) +
  scale_y_continuous(
    labels = function(x) paste0(x, "%"),
    breaks = seq(0, 100, by = 20)
  ) +
  theme_classic(base_size = 12) +
  theme(
    panel.grid.major = element_blank(),
    panel.grid.minor = element_blank()
  )

```

```{r}
library(ggplot2)
library(dplyr)

# Create heatmap showing individual participant responses
# Option 1: Basic heatmap
ggplot(participant_summary, aes(x = pejorative_type, y = reorder(submission_id, group_response_rate), fill = group_response_rate)) +
  geom_tile(color = "white", size = 0.1) +
  scale_fill_gradient2(
    low = "white", 
    mid = "lightblue", 
    high = "darkblue",
    midpoint = 50,
    name = "Group Response\nRate (%)",
    labels = function(x) paste0(x, "%")
  ) +
  labs(
    x = "Pejorative Type",
    y = "Participant ID",
    title = "Individual Participant Response Patterns by Pejorative Type"
  ) +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(
    axis.text.y = element_blank(),  # Hide individual participant IDs for cleaner look
    axis.ticks.y = element_blank(),
    panel.grid = element_blank(),
    plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5)
  )

# Option 2: Clustered heatmap (groups similar participants together)
library(tidyr)

# Create wide format for clustering
participant_wide <- participant_summary %>%
  pivot_wider(names_from = pejorative_type, values_from = group_response_rate, values_fill = 0)

# Calculate participant clusters based on response patterns
participant_matrix <- as.matrix(participant_wide[, -1])  # Remove submission_id column
rownames(participant_matrix) <- participant_wide$submission_id

# Perform hierarchical clustering
participant_clusters <- hclust(dist(participant_matrix))
participant_order <- rownames(participant_matrix)[participant_clusters$order]

# Add cluster order to data
participant_summary$participant_order <- factor(participant_summary$submission_id, levels = participant_order)

# Create clustered heatmap
ggplot(participant_summary, aes(x = pejorative_type, y = participant_order, fill = group_response_rate)) +
  geom_tile(color = "white", size = 0.1) +
  scale_fill_gradient2(
    low = "white", 
    mid = "lightblue", 
    high = "darkblue",
    midpoint = 50,
    name = "Group Response\nRate (%)",
    labels = function(x) paste0(x, "%")
  ) +
  labs(
    x = "Pejorative Type",
    y = "Participants (Clustered by Similarity)",
    title = "Individual Participant Response Patterns by Pejorative Type",
    subtitle = "Participants clustered by response similarity"
  ) +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(
    axis.text.y = element_blank(),  # Hide individual participant IDs
    axis.ticks.y = element_blank(),
    panel.grid = element_blank(),
    plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5),
    plot.subtitle = element_text(hjust = 0.5)
  )

# Option 3: Heatmap with participant gender information
# First, get participant gender information
participant_gender <- data_exp %>%
  select(submission_id, participant_gender) %>%
  distinct()

# Merge with summary data
participant_summary_with_gender <- participant_summary %>%
  left_join(participant_gender, by = "submission_id")

# Create heatmap faceted by gender
ggplot(participant_summary_with_gender, aes(x = pejorative_type, y = reorder(submission_id, group_response_rate), fill = group_response_rate)) +
  geom_tile(color = "white", size = 0.1) +
  scale_fill_gradient2(
    low = "white", 
    mid = "lightblue", 
    high = "darkblue",
    midpoint = 50,
    name = "Group Response\nRate (%)",
    labels = function(x) paste0(x, "%")
  ) +
  facet_wrap(~participant_gender, scales = "free_y", labeller = labeller(participant_gender = c("female" = "Female Participants", "male" = "Male Participants"))) +
  labs(
    x = "Pejorative Type",
    y = "Participants",
    title = "Individual Participant Response Patterns by Pejorative Type and Gender"
  ) +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(
    axis.text.y = element_blank(),
    axis.ticks.y = element_blank(),
    panel.grid = element_blank(),
    plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5),
    strip.text = element_text(size = 11)
  )
```

```{r}
library(dplyr)
library(tidyr)

# Create participant-level response rates for each pejorative type
participant_by_pejorative <- data_exp %>%
  group_by(submission_id, pejorative_type) %>%
  summarize(group_response_rate = mean(response_binary), .groups = "drop") %>%
  pivot_wider(names_from = pejorative_type, values_from = group_response_rate, values_fill = 0)

# Calculate correlation matrix
correlation_matrix <- participant_by_pejorative %>%
  select(-submission_id) %>%
  cor(use = "complete.obs")

print("Correlation Matrix:")
print(round(correlation_matrix, 3))

# Test significance of specific correlations using the correct column names
# Correlation between SLUR and GOI
cor_test_slur_goi <- cor.test(participant_by_pejorative$SLUR, participant_by_pejorative$GOI)
print("Correlation between SLUR and GOI:")
print(cor_test_slur_goi)

# Correlation between SLUR and COI  
cor_test_slur_coi <- cor.test(participant_by_pejorative$SLUR, participant_by_pejorative$COI)
print("Correlation between SLUR and COI:")
print(cor_test_slur_coi)

# Correlation between GOI and COI
cor_test_goi_coi <- cor.test(participant_by_pejorative$GOI, participant_by_pejorative$COI)
print("Correlation between GOI and COI:")
print(cor_test_goi_coi)

# Create scatterplots
library(ggplot2)

# SLUR vs GOI
ggplot(participant_by_pejorative, aes(x = SLUR, y = GOI)) +
  geom_point(alpha = 0.6, size = 2) +
  geom_smooth(method = "lm", se = TRUE, color = "red") +
  labs(
    x = "Group Response Rate for SLUR",
    y = "Group Response Rate for GOI",
    title = "Correlation between SLUR and GOI Response Rates",
    subtitle = paste("r =", round(cor(participant_by_pejorative$SLUR, participant_by_pejorative$GOI), 3))
  ) +
  scale_x_continuous(labels = function(x) paste0(x*100, "%")) +
  scale_y_continuous(labels = function(x) paste0(x*100, "%")) +
  theme_minimal()

# SLUR vs COI
ggplot(participant_by_pejorative, aes(x = SLUR, y = COI)) +
  geom_point(alpha = 0.6, size = 2) +
  geom_smooth(method = "lm", se = TRUE, color = "red") +
  labs(
    x = "Group Response Rate for SLUR",
    y = "Group Response Rate for COI",
    title = "Correlation between SLUR and COI Response Rates",
    subtitle = paste("r =", round(cor(participant_by_pejorative$SLUR, participant_by_pejorative$COI), 3))
  ) +
  scale_x_continuous(labels = function(x) paste0(x*100, "%")) +
  scale_y_continuous(labels = function(x) paste0(x*100, "%")) +
  theme_minimal()
```

```{r}
library(dplyr)

# Split participants using 62.5% as the cutoff threshold
# Exactly 62.5% counts as "Low"
cutoff_threshold <- 0.625
participant_slur_classification <- participant_by_pejorative %>%
  mutate(
    slur_group_responder = ifelse(SLUR > cutoff_threshold, "High (>62.5%)", "Low (≤62.5%)")
  )

# Check the distribution
table(participant_slur_classification$slur_group_responder)

# Calculate GOI response rates for each group
goi_by_slur_group <- participant_slur_classification %>%
  group_by(slur_group_responder) %>%
  summarise(
    n_participants = n(),
    mean_goi_rate = mean(GOI) * 100,
    sd_goi_rate = sd(GOI) * 100,
    median_goi_rate = median(GOI) * 100,
    .groups = "drop"
  )

print("GOI response rates by SLUR group response behavior (62.5% threshold):")
print(goi_by_slur_group)

# Statistical test
slur_high_goi <- participant_slur_classification$GOI[participant_slur_classification$slur_group_responder == "High (>62.5%)"]
slur_low_goi <- participant_slur_classification$GOI[participant_slur_classification$slur_group_responder == "Low (≤62.5%)"]

if(length(slur_high_goi) > 0 && length(slur_low_goi) > 0) {
  t_test_result <- t.test(slur_high_goi, slur_low_goi)
  print("T-test comparing GOI rates (>62.5% vs ≤62.5% SLUR responders):")
  print(t_test_result)
  
  # Effect size (Cohen's d)
  pooled_sd <- sqrt(((length(slur_high_goi) - 1) * var(slur_high_goi) + 
                     (length(slur_low_goi) - 1) * var(slur_low_goi)) / 
                    (length(slur_high_goi) + length(slur_low_goi) - 2))
  
  cohens_d <- (mean(slur_high_goi) - mean(slur_low_goi)) / pooled_sd
  
  cat("\nEffect size (Cohen's d):", round(cohens_d, 3), "\n")
  
  # Summary for manuscript
  cat("\nSummary for manuscript:\n")
  cat("Participants with high SLUR group response rates (>62.5%, n =", length(slur_high_goi), 
      ") had a mean GOI group response rate of", round(mean(slur_high_goi) * 100, 1), 
      "% compared to", round(mean(slur_low_goi) * 100, 1), 
      "% for those with low SLUR group response rates (≤62.5%, n =", length(slur_low_goi), 
      "), t(", round(t_test_result$parameter, 1), ") =", round(t_test_result$statistic, 3), 
      ", p =", round(t_test_result$p.value, 3), 
      ", Cohen's d =", round(cohens_d, 3), "\n")
} else {
  cat("Not enough participants in one or both groups for statistical testing\n")
  cat("High SLUR group size:", length(slur_high_goi), "\n")
  cat("Low SLUR group size:", length(slur_low_goi), "\n")
}

# Show the overall correlation as well
cat("\nOverall correlation between SLUR and GOI: r =", 
    round(cor(participant_by_pejorative$SLUR, participant_by_pejorative$GOI), 3), 
    ", p =", round(cor.test(participant_by_pejorative$SLUR, participant_by_pejorative$GOI)$p.value, 3), "\n")
```

```{r}
library(dplyr)
library(ggplot2)
library(tidyr)
library(viridis)

# Calculate proportion of "Group" responses for each pejorative word
pejorative_word_stats <- data_exp %>%
  group_by(pejorative_type, pejorative_word) %>%
  summarize(
    total = n(),
    group_responses = sum(response_type == "Group"),
    individual_responses = sum(response_type != "Group"),
    group_pct = mean(response_type == "Group") * 100,
    .groups = "drop"
  ) %>%
  arrange(pejorative_type, desc(group_pct))

# Print the statistics
print(pejorative_word_stats)

# CHI-SQUARE TESTS: Within-category variation
print("=== CHI-SQUARE TESTS FOR WITHIN-CATEGORY VARIATION ===")

for(pej_type in unique(pejorative_word_stats$pejorative_type)) {
  cat("\n--- Testing variation within", pej_type, "words ---\n")
  
  # Get data for this pejorative type
  type_data <- data_exp %>%
    filter(pejorative_type == pej_type) %>%
    select(pejorative_word, response_type)
  
  # Create contingency table
  contingency_table <- table(type_data$pejorative_word, type_data$response_type)
  
  # Show the contingency table
  cat("Contingency table:\n")
  print(contingency_table)
  
  # Only run chi-square if we have enough words (>1) and some variation
  if(nrow(contingency_table) > 1 && sum(contingency_table) > 0) {
    
    # Check if we have enough expected frequencies (all cells >= 5)
    chi_result <- chisq.test(contingency_table)
    
    cat("Chi-square test results:\n")
    cat("χ² =", round(chi_result$statistic, 3), "\n")
    cat("df =", chi_result$parameter, "\n")
    cat("p-value =", format.pval(chi_result$p.value, digits = 3), "\n")
    cat("Interpretation:", ifelse(chi_result$p.value < 0.05, 
                                "Significant variation between words", 
                                "No significant variation between words"), "\n")
    
    # Show percentages for each word in this category
    word_percentages <- pejorative_word_stats %>%
      filter(pejorative_type == pej_type) %>%
      select(pejorative_word, group_pct) %>%
      arrange(desc(group_pct))
    
    cat("Group-derogation percentages by word:\n")
    for(i in 1:nrow(word_percentages)) {
      cat("  ", word_percentages$pejorative_word[i], ": ", 
          round(word_percentages$group_pct[i], 1), "%\n", sep = "")
    }
    
  } else {
    cat("Cannot perform chi-square test (insufficient data or only one word)\n")
  }
  
  cat("\n")
}

# Create the existing plots...
word_plot <- ggplot(pejorative_word_stats, 
                   aes(x = reorder(pejorative_word, group_pct), 
                       y = group_pct, 
                       fill = pejorative_type)) +
  geom_bar(stat = "identity", alpha = 0.8) +
  geom_text(aes(label = sprintf("%.1f%%", group_pct)), 
            hjust = -0.1, size = 3.5) +
  scale_fill_viridis_d() +
  labs(
    title = "Proportion of Group-Directed Responses by Pejorative Word",
    subtitle = "Words ordered by percentage within each category",
    x = "Pejorative Word",
    y = "Group-Directed Responses (%)",
    fill = "Pejorative Type"
  ) +
  coord_flip() +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(
    legend.position = "top",
    plot.title = element_text(face = "bold"),
    axis.title = element_text(face = "bold")
  ) +
  facet_wrap(~pejorative_type, scales = "free_y")

# Create a second visualization with words grouped by category
category_plot <- ggplot(pejorative_word_stats, 
                       aes(x = pejorative_type, y = group_pct, color = pejorative_word)) +
  geom_point(aes(size = total), alpha = 0.7, position = position_jitter(width = 0.2, height = 0)) +
  scale_color_viridis_d() +
  scale_size_continuous(range = c(3, 8)) +
  labs(
    title = "Group-Directed Responses by Pejorative Word and Category",
    x = "Pejorative Type",
    y = "Group-Directed Responses (%)",
    color = "Pejorative Word",
    size = "Number of\nObservations"
  ) +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(
    plot.title = element_text(face = "bold"),
    axis.title = element_text(face = "bold"),
    legend.title = element_text(face = "bold")
  )

# Display the plots
print(word_plot)
print(category_plot)

# Optional: Create a heatmap for an alternative view
if(length(unique(pejorative_word_stats$pejorative_word)) >= 5) {
  heatmap_data <- pejorative_word_stats %>%
    select(pejorative_type, pejorative_word, group_pct)
  
  heatmap_plot <- ggplot(heatmap_data, 
                        aes(x = pejorative_type, y = pejorative_word, fill = group_pct)) +
    geom_tile(color = "white") +
    geom_text(aes(label = sprintf("%.1f%%", group_pct)), color = "white", size = 3) +
    scale_fill_viridis_c() +
    labs(
      title = "Heatmap of Group-Directed Responses by Pejorative Word",
      x = "Pejorative Type",
      y = "Pejorative Word",
      fill = "Group %"
    ) +
    theme_minimal() +
    theme(
      plot.title = element_text(face = "bold"),
      axis.title = element_text(face = "bold")
    )
  
  print(heatmap_plot)
}
```
